name: Grilled Tomatillo Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
For green salsa:
1. Husk, core and quarter tomatillos.
2. Peel, pit and cut avocado into 1-inch pieces.
3. Finely chop tomatillos, tomato and garlic in processor, using on/off turns.
4. Transfer mixture to heavy medium saucepan.
5. Simmer until beginning to soften, stirring occasionally, about 3 minutes.
6. Pour mixture into blender.
7. Add avocado, chili, cilantro and salt. Blend until smooth, stopping occasionally to scrape down sides of blender, about 3 minutes.
8. (Can be prepared 1 day ahead and refrigerated.) Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled.
To prepare the marinade:
1. Soak mesquite chips in water to cover for 30 minutes and drain.
2. Prepare barbecue grill with white-hot coals (or set gas grill on high).
3. Combine oil, butter, chili powder and lemon juice in small saucepan.
4. Stir over medium heat until butter melts.
To prepare shrimp:
1. Peel shrimp (leave tail on) and devein.
2. Place in non-aluminum bowl.
3. Add marinade, mixing to coat.
4. Marinate for 15 minutes, turning occasionally.
5. Oil barbecue rack.
6. Add mesquite to coals.
7. Thread shrimp on skewers.
8. Grill until shrimp turn opaque, about 1 1/2 minutes on each side.
9. (Can be prepared 30 minutes ahead. Wrap in foil and keep at room temperature.) Serve with green salsa. Available at Latin American markets.
